prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `f21745ec`.
codex_response:
Implemented `f21745ec` as a new ARC2 task package. The rule is: find the one colored rectangle whose interior is already populated, take its full occupancy mask inside the bounding box, copy that mask into every rectangle with the same outer dimensions using each rectangle’s own color, and erase all differently sized rectangles.

I discarded the Sonnet hint and used the Opus hint. Sonnet was wrong because it described a generic diagonal fill; the official examples instead copy an exact template mask. The generator matches the family by producing square black grids with 2-4 same-sized frames, exactly one pre-patterned template, 1-2 differently sized hollow distractors, unique colors, and spaced non-overlapping layout. No new shared primitive was needed.

Verification passed end-to-end: official examples `4/4`, generated examples `1000/1000`, and I visually checked the preview sheet against the originals.
